samples.gvd:
module asyn_fifo(
    input wclk,
    input rclk,
    input rstn,
    input winc,
    input rinc,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata,
    output wfull,
    output rempty,
    output [WIDTH-1:0] rdata
);
  // Insert your code here
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Active-low reset signal for both read and write operations. Write increment signal. Triggered when a write operation is complete. Read increment signal. Triggered when a read operation is complete. Write data input. Data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. Data read from the FIFO. Width of the FIFO. Depth of the FIFO.

1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule:

<module id="dual_port_RAM"> <description>Implement a dual-port RAM module.&description> <parameter_description> <param id="WIDTH" value="8"/> <param id="DEPTH" value="16"/> </parameter_description> <input id="wclk"/> <input id="wenc"/> <input id="waddr" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)"/> <input id="wdata" width_description="WIDTH"/> <input id="rclk"/> <input id="renc"/> <input id="raddr" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)"/> <output id="rdata" width_description="WIDTH"/> </module>

2. In the asyn_fifo module, connect the ports of the dual-port RAM module according to the control signals and pointer values:

<module id="asyn_fifo"> <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full.

3. Implement the write pointer logic:

The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address
